text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A useful method is setting SMART goals, which are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," a SMART goal would be "increase website traffic by 20% in three months." To track progress, break the project into milestones, such as completing the design phase by a certain date. Each milestone should have clear deliverables, like a written report or a prototype. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved, such as team members or clients. This helps avoid confusion and keeps the project moving in the right direction.
